summary: Crystal‑clear acoustic set, tight electric flow, Rosalie & Not Fade Away shine
blurb: The October 11 1980 Warfield show delivers a pristine acoustic segment highlighted by Rosalie McFall and Heaven Help The Fool, then rolls into an energetic electric second set with a killer lead‑into Not Fade Away. Jerry’s guitar and Phil’s low‑end bass are standout throughout.
review: On October 11, 1980 the Dead took the Warfield by storm, opening with an acoustic set that many reviewers called “crystal‑clear” and “stellar.”  The soundboard capture of this half showcases a soaring Jerry vocal on Rosalie McFall, sweet phrasing on Heaven Help The Fool, and a beautifully voiced Cassidy that keeps fans humming long after the night.  The electric portion maintains that momentum; Phil’s bass lines drive a powerful lead‑into Not Fade Away while the band rides a tight groove through China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and into an expansive Space.  Even with some recordings missing the final encore, the overall vibe is timeless, making this one of the stronger Warfield outings of the early ’80s.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the acoustic clarity, standout songs (Rosalie McFall, Cassidy, Not Fade Away) and overall vibe; only one negative note about a date error and a few mixed comments about missing tape portions, yielding strong positive sentiment across the majority of sources.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the acoustic set with standout performances of Rosalie McFall, Heaven Help The Fool and Cassidy; complete, high‑fidelity source.

key_highlights: Pristine acoustic segment captured on a crystal‑clear SBD, featuring Rosalie McFall and Heaven Help The Fool.
Rich, bright guitar tone on Cassidy and Deep Elem Blues adds depth to the early set.
High‑energy electric second set with an exhilarating lead‑into Not Fade Away and tight low‑end bass work from Phil.
Overall timeless vibe despite a few recordings missing the final encore.

band_performance.Jerry: His vocal presence is front‑and‑center (“lots of Jerry”, “sweet voice”) and his guitar shines with a crisp, rich tone that reviewers repeatedly praised.
band_performance.Phil: Mixed feedback: some note his bass gets buried in the acoustic mix, but others celebrate his toe‑to‑toe low‑end licks that drive the electric set, especially into Not Fade Away.
